title: Area Under Curves Using Integration: An Overview
summary: Integration is a fundamental concept in calculus that calculates the area under curves. This method provides accurate results for both simple and complex areas.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: The 'area under curve' refers to finding the space enclosed between a function, its graph, and horizontal lines drawn at various points on that same axis.
key_points: Area represents accumulation of quantities like speed or temperature over time

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: Integration is a process where we calculate the limit as the width of subintervals approaches zero, effectively summing up infinitesimally small rectangles underneath the curve.
key_points: Use definite integrals to find exact area values

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Calculating areas using integration is crucial in many real-world problems like physics, engineering and economics where accumulation of quantities over time or space are required.
key_points: Applicable for both discrete and continuous scenarios

key_facts: The area under a curve can be computed using integration, which is central to calculus.
